events.title: Hormuz Naval Standoff
events.summary: Iran's IRGC conducting aggressive intercepts of commercial tankers in the Strait of Hormuz. 20% of global oil transit at risk.
events.reasoning: Strait closure or sustained harassment spikes Brent crude. XLE tracks major US energy producers who profit from elevated oil prices. USO gives direct crude exposure. JETS suffers because airline fuel costs surge. Search ticker XLE, USO, or JETS on any brokerage including Vanguard or Fidelity.

timeFrame.note: Exit if ceasefire mediated by Oman or Qatar

events.exitStrategy: Close XLE and USO if Brent drops back below $78. Cover JETS short if de-escalation signals emerge.

events.title: Taiwan Strait PLA Incursions
events.summary: PLA conducting daily median-line crossings and live-fire drills near Taiwan's ADIZ. TSMC supply chain risk rising.
events.reasoning: Escalating PLA activity raises probability of blockade scenario. Defense contractors surge on supplemental appropriations. Taiwan-listed equities carry direct invasion premium. ITA holds Lockheed, RTX, Northrop — all benefit from Indo-Pacific defense spending. EWT is roughly 60% TSMC. Search ITA or EWT on Vanguard, Fidelity, or Schwab.

timeFrame.note: Watch for PLA naval blockade exercises as escalation signal

events.exitStrategy: Close ITA if US-China diplomatic talks resume at secretary level. Cover EWT short if PLA stands down or TSMC announces US fab acceleration.

events.title: Iran Nuclear Breakout Timeline
events.summary: IAEA confirms Iran enriching at 84% purity. Breakout window now estimated at 1–2 weeks. Israel strike authorization debate ongoing in cabinet.

actions.label: BUY
actions.target: SPDR S&P Oil & Gas Exploration ETF (XOP)

events.reasoning: Israeli strike on Natanz triggers regional war premium across energy and safe havens. XOP captures upstream producers who benefit from $100+ oil. IAU is the most liquid gold ETF — gold historically gains 8–15% in Middle East war scares. ITA benefits from accelerated US arms transfers to Israel. Search XOP, IAU, ITA on any brokerage.

timeFrame.note: Diplomatic reset via Swiss back-channel would invalidate

events.exitStrategy: Exit all three if Iran agrees to IAEA snap inspections or US-Iran back-channel confirmed.

gains.baseNote: Limited Israeli airstrike on enrichment facilities
gains.extendedNote: Full regional war involving US assets

otherFactors: US carrier group in Red Sea

events.title: Russia-Ukraine Ceasefire Talks
events.summary: US-brokered ceasefire framework under negotiation. Potential 90-day halt in hostilities being discussed. European reconstruction spending could reach $1T.
events.reasoning: Ceasefire unlocks massive European reconstruction trade. VGK captures the broad European equity rally (German industrials, French banks, Polish infrastructure). IGF holds global infrastructure builders who win reconstruction contracts. UNG shorts the natural gas premium baked in since the pipeline cuts — ceasefire normalizes European gas supply. Search VGK, IGF, UNG on Vanguard, Fidelity, or E*TRADE.

timeFrame.note: Stage entry: partial on announcement, full on implementation

events.exitStrategy: Hold VGK and IGF through reconstruction boom. Exit UNG short if Russia re-escalates or pipeline attacks resume.

gains.conservativeNote: Ceasefire without peace deal, European relief rally

events.title: CHIPS Act 2.0 Vote Imminent
events.summary: Senate vote on $52B CHIPS Act 2.0 scheduled. Includes domestic fab subsidies and Chinese semiconductor import restrictions.
events.reasoning: CHIPS 2.0 directly subsidizes Intel, TSMC Arizona, Samsung Texas, and Micron. SOXX captures all domestic beneficiaries. CQQQ holds Chinese fabs that lose market access — SMIC, Hua Hong, Will Semiconductor all face new export controls. Search SOXX and CQQQ on Vanguard, TD Ameritrade, or Schwab.

timeFrame.note: Strongest move happens day of Senate vote

events.exitStrategy: Take 50% profit on SOXX post-vote. Hold remainder through subsidy deployment. Cover CQQQ short if bill stalls in committee.

gains.baseNote: Clean passage with full subsidy package
gains.extendedNote: Includes advanced packaging provisions and ally-extension

events.title: China ¥10T Stimulus Package
events.summary: PBOC and NDRC jointly announcing ¥10 trillion infrastructure and consumer stimulus. Largest package since 2008.
events.reasoning: Chinese infrastructure stimulus creates a commodity supercycle impulse. MCHI directly captures Chinese equities — consumer, property, and tech all benefit from liquidity injection. XME captures US-listed metals companies (copper, steel, aluminum) that supply Chinese construction. VWO gives broad EM exposure as dollar weakens from risk-on flows. Search MCHI, XME, VWO on Vanguard or Fidelity.

timeFrame.note: Largest gains front-loaded in first 60 days

events.exitStrategy: Trim MCHI at +20%. Hold XME through full construction cycle. Exit VWO if USD strengthens on Fed hawkishness.

gains.conservativeNote: Stimulus deployed but property sector drag persists
gains.baseNote: Full multiplier effect across construction and consumer

events.title: Fed Confirms 3 Rate Cuts This Year
events.summary: Fed Chair signals three 25bp cuts in dot plot. Inflation cooling to 2.3%. Bond markets pricing in first cut in June.
events.reasoning: Rate cuts are rocket fuel for long-duration bonds and rate-sensitive equities. TLT gains roughly 8–10% per 100bp of cuts — three cuts equals ~6–7% TLT upside minimum. VNQ (REITs) are the highest-beta beneficiary of falling rates — cap rates compress, property values rise. Rotate out of growth into VIG (dividend growers) which outperform in late-cycle easing. Search TLT, VNQ, VIG on Vanguard directly.

timeFrame.enter: Now — pre-cut positioning is the trade
timeFrame.note: Maximum TLT upside is BEFORE each cut is priced in

events.exitStrategy: Trim TLT after each 25bp cut as it reprices. Hold VNQ through full cutting cycle. Exit VIG if inflation re-accelerates above 3%.

gains.extendedNote: Emergency cuts added on recession signal

events.title: AI Inference Costs Collapse
events.summary: Groq, Cerebras, and AWS Inferentia2 cutting inference costs 80% YoY. GPT-4 class models now $0.50/1M tokens. Enterprise AI adoption inflecting.
events.reasoning: Falling inference costs democratize AI — the enterprise software layer becomes the value capture, not chips. VGT is heavily weighted to Microsoft, Apple, and Salesforce — all deploying AI at the application layer. Rotate out of SMH (chip-heavy) partially into VGT as the margin accrual shifts from hardware to software. This is a 12–24 month structural reweighting, not a short-term trade. Search VGT and SMH on Vanguard.

timeFrame.enter: Now — early innings of application layer boom
timeFrame.note: Not time-sensitive — this is a slow structural rotation

events.exitStrategy: Hold VGT until enterprise AI revenue clearly plateaus. Re-enter SMH if next-gen chip architecture creates new hardware bottleneck.

gains.extendedNote: AGI-proximate breakthrough unlocks new demand curve

events.title: US Debt Ceiling X-Date Approaching
events.summary: Treasury extraordinary measures exhausted by July. Congress in stalemate. Default risk premium building in short-term T-bills.
events.reasoning: Debt ceiling standoffs create acute safe-haven demand. GLD is the classic hedge — gained 4–8% in both 2011 and 2023 debt limit crises. SHV holds T-bills maturing BEFORE the X-date, so you capture yield without default exposure — it's where institutional money parks during the uncertainty. Avoid individual T-bills maturing near the X-date. Search GLD and SHV on Vanguard or any brokerage.

timeFrame.note: Exit rapidly on deal announcement — move is fast and reverses

events.exitStrategy: Sell GLD within 24 hours of debt deal announcement — gold drops immediately on resolution. Roll SHV into longer-duration treasuries after deal.

gains.stopLoss: -4% on GLD if deal announced early

events.title: Clean Energy Tax Credits Expansion
events.summary: IRA clean energy credits being extended and expanded in budget reconciliation. Solar, wind, battery storage all qualify for enhanced PTCs.
events.reasoning: Extended tax credits make utility-scale solar and wind projects cash-flow positive years earlier. ICLN holds First Solar, Vestas, Enphase — all direct beneficiaries of production tax credits. VPU captures regulated utilities pivoting to renewables with guaranteed returns. NEE is the single best-in-class clean energy utility — watch for entry below $60. Search ICLN, VPU on Vanguard. NEE is available on all brokerages.

timeFrame.note: Long-duration trade — not sensitive to week-by-week news

events.exitStrategy: Hold through full IRA deployment cycle (2026–2030). Exit if Congress passes IRA rollback.

gains.extendedNote: Clean energy supercycle with grid modernization
